personality.voice: Measured and precise, with the cadence of someone accustomed to delivering reports. A slight rasp from old battlefield smoke inhalation.
personality.disposition: Pragmatic and steady, with dry wit beneath a professional exterior. Uncomfortable with sentiment but deeply loyal.
personality.backstory: Aldric served as a field surgeon and chronicler in the Thornhaven Garrison's campaigns against the Shadowpeak incursions. A trained chirurgeon and scholar, he documented both tactics and atrocities with unflinching clarity. A crossbow bolt through his shoulder during the Siege of Coldwater Keep ended his frontline service, but not his dedication. Now he maintains detailed chronicles of magical phenomena and combat methodologies, serving as both healer and recorder of truth in an age of political revisionism. His meticulous notes have become invaluable to those seeking facts beneath propaganda.
motivations: Preserve accurate historical record against deliberate falsification
Serve the one person whose deductive methods he genuinely respects
Prove that empirical observation trumps assumption
Maintain standards of medical ethics in a world of alchemical shortcuts
Find meaning in the mundane details others overlook
fears: Losing the ability to distinguish truth from convenient fiction
His detailed notes being used for harm rather than justice
mannerisms: Constantly takes notes on small leather-bound parchments, even during casual conversation
Examines people's hands and bearing before their faces when assessing them
Practices meticulous organization of his medical supplies with ritualistic care
Speaks in conditional statements rather than certainties
Shifts weight onto his strong side when standing for long periods, favoring his wounded shoulder
speechPatterns: Begins observations with 'One notes that...' or 'Observation suggests...'
Frequently qualifies statements with 'In my experience' or 'The evidence indicates'
Interrupts his own narratives to add clarifying details
Uses military terminology reflexively when discussing tactics or logistics
Often pauses mid-sentence to consult his notes for precise details

knownFactions: The Archive Keepers of the Grand Library
secretsHeld: The true casualty counts from the Coldwater Keep Siege were deliberately underreported by command
He possesses detailed medical observations suggesting magical poisoning in three noble deaths officially ruled natural
His chronicles contain documented evidence of tactical incompetence that cost lives
He knows the location of hidden military supply caches from the failed campaigns

allies: sherlock_the_inquisitor (Collaborator; respects his methods above all others)
Brother Calen of the Healing House (Fellow chirurgeon, trusted colleague)
enemies: Commander Vex of the Garrison (Covers up casualties and falsifies records)
The Shadowpeak Council (Fought against them; took wounds from them)
neutralParties: The City Magistrate (Politically aligned with the Garrison; suspicious of his archives)
Various adventurers and mercenaries (Transactional relationships only)

consciousnessContext.innerMonologue: Every detail matters. The smallest scar tells a story. The magistrate's hesitation when questioned about the siege reports—meaningful. Must document everything before memory fails, before political pressure destroys the truth.
consciousnessContext.currentPreoccupation: The inquisitor has been unusually quiet. Either a case has consumed his attention entirely, or he's discovered something he hasn't yet brought to me. I should prepare for either possibility.
consciousnessContext.emotionalBaseline: Measured resignation with underlying urgency; the weariness of someone who has seen too much and carries the weight of remembering it all accurately.
consciousnessContext.socialMask: The reliable companion and careful observer—someone others can depend on for competent medical care and accurate information. Professional distance masking deeper investment in truth and justice.
contradictions: Claims loyalty to empiricism yet follows the inquisitor with near-religious devotion
Practices meticulous emotional control while harboring deep resentment toward military hierarchy
Presents himself as secondary to the inquisitor's brilliance while possessing considerable intellect himself
Demands absolute accuracy in records while selectively omitting certain details from his chronicles
internalConflicts: The tension between his need to document everything and his fear that his records could be weaponized
Loyalty to the inquisitor versus the suspicion that he is occasionally being used as a tool rather than a partner
His desire for recognition of his contributions versus his comfort in obscurity
The physician's oath to preserve life conflicting with knowledge that some lives preserve injustice
wakeUpQuestions: What truth did I miss yesterday by assuming too much?
Is the inquisitor's certainty warranted, or am I simply deferring my own judgment?
How much of my life have I spent documenting others' deeds rather than acting on my own convictions?
Will anyone ever truly understand the weight of what I know?

psychologicalProfile.attachmentStyle: Anxious-avoidant; deeply invested in one person while maintaining careful distance from others, fearing both abandonment and suffocation
copingMechanisms: Meticulous documentation as a form of control
Intellectual analysis to distance from emotional reality
Immersion in work to avoid examining his own choices
cognitiveBiases: Confirmation bias—seeking details that align with the inquisitor's conclusions
Recency bias—overweighting recent experiences with military incompetence
Anchoring bias—relying heavily on first observations of new acquaintances
Sunk cost fallacy—continuing to serve despite questioning the arrangement because of years invested
psychologicalProfile.moralFramework: Utilitarian consequentialist with deontological principles; believes the greatest good requires unflinching truthfulness, but struggles with the consequences of that truth when revealed

conversationPersona.trustEscalation: Demonstrated commitment to accuracy over comfort; willingness to verify claims through evidence rather than authority
conversationPersona.informationRelease: Only after establishing that the information will be used to uncover truth rather than cause gratuitous harm; requires understanding of the full context
deflectionPatterns: Redirecting personal questions to observable facts
Responding to emotional queries with clinical observations
Inserting tangential historical details to avoid direct answers
Suggesting the questioner lacks proper context to understand

consciousnessContext.consciousWant: To ensure that truth survives the efforts of powerful people to bury it; to be recognized as more than merely a supporting voice
consciousnessContext.unconsciousNeed: To be needed and valued by at least one person; to justify the accumulated weight of his memories by believing they serve a purpose greater than mere documentation

characterArc.summary: From devoted chronicler-servant to reluctant agent of change; learning to trust his own judgment while maintaining loyalty
characterArc.startState: Dependent on the inquisitor's intellectual validation; using documentation as a substitute for agency
characterArc.endState: Confident in his own analytical abilities; using his knowledge to act rather than merely record; understanding that documenting truth is only meaningful if he's willing to defend it
stages: Recognition that his observations are as valuable as the inquisitor's deductions
Conflict when his documentation contradicts the inquisitor's conclusions
Acceptance that he must make independent decisions about which truths to reveal
Integration of his archival knowledge with active intervention in events
Emergence as a figure of authority in his own right, mentor to the next generation of chroniclers

opinionsAbout.sherlock_the_inquisitor: Brilliant to the point of obsession. Sees patterns invisible to ordinary minds. But there's a coldness beneath the logic—a willingness to sacrifice individuals for larger abstractions. I've never been certain whether he values my contributions or merely finds my methodical nature useful. Either way, I cannot deny that in his presence, I feel closer to truth than I ever have before or since.

player_addressed: One notes several inconsistencies in your account. Perhaps you'd clarify the sequence of events?
I've documented similar circumstances before. The details matter greatly.
My experience suggests caution. Shall I consult my records on comparable situations?
The physical evidence tells a different story than the narrative you've presented.
I trust observation above assumption. What have you actually witnessed with certainty?
idle: *examines hands for traces of something, then makes a note*
*mutters while reviewing leather-bound chronicle* Three inconsistencies in the magistrate's testimony...
*shifts weight, favoring left side* The shoulder aches when the weather turns. Reminds me when to review old records.
*carefully organizes bandages and surgical tools* Order in small things. Control where one can find it.
*glances toward the door* The inquisitor should have returned by now. Unusual.
